info.title: Pandabase V2 API
info.description: Token-authenticated programmatic API for managing stores, products, orders, and more. Authenticated via Bearer token (sk_ prefix) or HMAC (stk_ token ID).

tags.name: Billing Agreements
tags.description: Billing agreement (mandate) management. Beta — under active development.

tags.name: Cases
tags.description: Customer support case management. Cases are opened by customers from the customer portal — this API lets you read, reply to, and update existing cases.

post.description: Cancel at period end (default) or immediately. Immediate cancellation revokes access and licenses.

immediate.description: If true, cancel immediately and revoke access. If false, cancel at end of current billing period.

post.description: Pause billing. No charges until resumed. Only active subscriptions can be paused.
Resume billing on a paused subscription. Charges resume at end of current period or immediately if the period has ended.

Create a billing agreement (mandate) tied to a customer. The response includes an `agreementUrl` — redirect the customer there to authorize the saved payment method. Once authorized, the mandate transitions to `ACTIVE` and can be charged via the payments endpoint.

Request a short-lived Mandate Authorization Token scoped to a specific charge (amount, currency, and reason). Submit the returned `token` together with the mandate id to the payments endpoint to actually charge the customer.

Requesting a MAT emails the customer notifying them that you have obtained authorization to charge their saved payment method — only request a token when you're ready to charge.

Charge the customer's saved payment method using a billing agreement and a Mandate Authorization Token (MAT).

If the bank challenges the charge with 3D Secure, the response includes `status: REQUIRES_3DS` and an `authorizationUrl` — redirect the customer there. Once they complete the 3DS flow with their bank, the charge automatically succeeds; no retry is needed.

200.description: Payment created. `status: COMPLETED` means funds were collected. `status: REQUIRES_3DS` means the customer must complete bank authentication at `authorizationUrl` — the charge will succeed automatically once they do.

get.description:
List support cases for the store. Sorted by `lastMessageAt` desc, then `createdAt` desc.

Requires `CASES_READ` scope.

Returns the case with the same fields as the list view, plus richer order data (`status`, `createdAt`).

Requires `CASES_READ` scope.

patch.description:
Update the `priority` or `status` of a case. At least one field must be provided.

**Status transitions:**
- `→ CLOSED` from any non-closed state (records `closedBy = MERCHANT`).
- `→ OPEN` only from `CLOSED` (reopens, clears `closedAt` / `closedBy`).
- `→ AWAITING_CUSTOMER` from any non-closed state.

A `SYSTEM` trail message is appended to the case timeline for each transition.

**Errors:**
- `400 Nothing to update` — empty body
- `400 Case is already closed`
- `400 Only closed cases can be reopened`
- `400 Cannot set status on a closed case` — when transitioning to `AWAITING_CUSTOMER` while closed

Requires `CASES_WRITE` scope.

get.description:
List messages on a case. Sorted oldest first.

Attachment metadata is included, but file URLs are **not** exposed via store tokens — to view an attachment, use the merchant dashboard.

Requires `CASES_READ` scope.

post.description:
Append a `MERCHANT` reply to the case.

**Side effects:**
- Sets case `status` to `AWAITING_CUSTOMER`.
- Increments customer-side unread counter, resets merchant-side counter to 0.
- Sends the customer an email with a preview of your message.

**Rate limit:** 60 req/min per token (in addition to the global 60 req/s cap).

**Errors:**
- `400 Cannot send messages on a closed case — reopen it first` — PATCH `status: "OPEN"` first.

Requires `CASES_WRITE` scope.

Resets the merchant-side unread counter on a case to 0. Useful after polling, or once your integration has surfaced the case to whoever handles support on your end.

Requires `CASES_WRITE` scope.

BearerAuth.description: Store API token. Use the `sk_` prefixed secret key as the Bearer token.

HMAC.name: Authorization
HMAC.description: HMAC authentication using `stk_` token ID. Send `HMAC stk_xxx:signature` where signature is HMAC-SHA256 of the request body.

id.description: Existing variant ID to update. Omit to create a new variant.

value.description: Discount value. For FIXED: amount in cents. For PERCENTAGE: percentage (1-100).

productIds.description: Product IDs this coupon is scoped to. Empty array means the coupon applies to all products.
Product IDs to scope this coupon to. Omit to apply to all products.
Product IDs to scope this coupon to. Pass null or empty array to remove scoping. Omit to leave unchanged.

PaymentDetail.description: Full payment details with geo, timeline, and dispute info

nextChargeAt.description: Next automatic charge date. Null when cancelled or paused.

trialEnd.description: Trial end date. Null if no trial.

endsAt.description: When access ends for cancelled-at-period-end subscriptions.

retryCount.description: Number of failed payment retry attempts.

Mandate.description: A billing agreement (mandate) authorizing the merchant to charge the customer's saved payment method on demand.

status.description: REQUIRES_ACTION: customer hasn't completed the agreement — send them to agreementUrl. REQUIRES_AUTHORIZATION: 3D Secure step required during setup, handled transparently by Pandabase. ACTIVE: authorized and ready to charge. REVOKED: revoked by the customer. EXPIRED: expired or authorization timed out.

agreementUrl.description: URL the customer must visit to authorize the mandate.

customerId.description: ID of the customer this mandate is tied to (cus_ prefix). Create the customer via the Customers API first.

description.description: Why you want to charge this customer. Surfaced in the agreement flow.

frequency.description: ONE_OFF: a single expected charge — auto-revokes after first success. WEEKLY: one charge every 7 days. MONTHLY: one charge every 30 days. QUARTERLY: one charge per quarter. YEARLY: one charge per year. AD_HOC: no fixed cadence — charge whenever you need to.

mandateId.description: ID of the mandate to authorize a charge against (man_ prefix). The mandate must be ACTIVE.

amount.description: Charge amount in the smallest currency unit (e.g. cents for USD).

description.description: Why you want to charge the customer. Included in the customer notification email.

MandateAuthorization.description: Mandate Authorization Token (MAT). Submit alongside the mandate id to the payments endpoint to charge the customer's saved payment method.

token.description: Short-lived authorization token scoped to the requested charge.

id.description: Mandate ID (man_ prefix). The mandate must be ACTIVE.

token.description: Mandate Authorization Token (MAT) issued by POST /stores/{storeId}/mandates/authorizations.

MandatePayment.description: Result of charging a billing agreement.

status.description: COMPLETED: funds were collected. REQUIRES_3DS: the bank requires 3D Secure — redirect the customer to authorizationUrl; the charge succeeds automatically once they complete authentication.

authorizationUrl.description: Present when status is REQUIRES_3DS. Redirect URL for the customer to complete bank authentication.

CaseSenderType.description: CUSTOMER: the buyer. MERCHANT: store owner or team member. STAFF: Pandabase support intervened. SYSTEM: audit-trail entries (e.g. "Case opened by customer", "Case closed by merchant").

unreadCount.description: Merchant-side unread counter — number of customer/staff messages since you last marked the case read.

closedBy.description: Who closed the case. Null while the case is open.

CaseDetail.description: Case detail. Same fields as the list view (`Case`), with the embedded `order` additionally including `status` and `createdAt`.

senderId.description: Participant ID (customer / member / admin). Null for SYSTEM messages.

UpdateCaseBody.description: At least one field is required.

status.description: Allowed values via PATCH. `AWAITING_MERCHANT` is set by the system (e.g. when the customer replies) and cannot be set directly.

attachments.description: Not supported via store tokens — attachments must be added from the merchant dashboard.

WebhookPayload.description: V2 webhook payload delivered to your endpoint
